name: QA 1616
description:
PCP QA Test No. 1616

pmlogger push testing



valgrind variant, see qa/1611 for the non-valgrind variant



check-group-include: pmlogger pmproxy pmlogpush



Copyright (c) 2025 Red Hat.  All Rights Reserved.



**To reproduce this test, please run:**

    build/ci/ci-run.py fedora42-container reproduce
descriptionHtml:
PCP QA Test No. 1616

pmlogger push testing

valgrind variant, see qa/1611 for the non-valgrind variant

check-group-include: pmlogger pmproxy pmlogpush

Copyright (c) 2025 Red Hat. All Rights Reserved.

To reproduce this test, please run:

build/ci/ci-run.py fedora42-container reproduce
statusMessage:
output mismatch (see 1616.out.bad)
2a3,12
> A dependency job for pmproxy.service failed. See 'journalctl -xe' for details.
> Hint: You are currently not seeing messages from other users and the system.
>       Users in groups 'adm', 'systemd-journal', 'wheel' can see all messages.
>       Pass -q to turn off this notice.
> No journal files were opened due to insufficient permissions.
> pmproxy failed to start on port 44322 after 20 seconds
> likely looking processes ...
> UID          PID    PPID  C STIME TTY          TIME CMD
> pmproxy logfile (/var/log/pcp/pmproxy/pmproxy.log) ...
> Log for pmproxy on 774de3eb2910 started Sat Jan  3 20:56:26 2026
4,25c14,47
< === Running pmlogger in remote recording mode
< === std out ===
< === std err ===
< === filtered valgrind report ===

< Command: pmlogger -c TMP.conf -T 5sec http://localhost:44322

< definitely lost: 0 bytes in 0 blocks
< indirectly lost: 0 bytes in 0 blocks
< ERROR SUMMARY: 0 errors from 0 contexts ...
< ## Found remote archive files
< REMOTE_ARCHIVE.0
< REMOTE_ARCHIVE.index
< REMOTE_ARCHIVE.meta
< ## with the following metrics

< disk.all.write
< hinv.ncpu
< hinv.ndisk

< kernel.all.pswitch
< sample.datasize
---
> pmproxy: PID = 1683070, PDU version = 2, user = pcp (991)
> pmproxy request port(s):
>   sts fd   port  family address
>   === ==== ===== ====== =======
>   ok    11       unix   /run/pcp/pmproxy.socket
>   ok    12 44322 inet   INADDR_ANY
>   ok    13 44322 ipv6   INADDR_ANY
>   ok    14 44323 inet   INADDR_ANY
>   ok    15 44323 ipv6   INADDR_ANY
> [Sat Jan  3 20:56:26] pmproxy(1683070) Info: Key server slots, schema version setup
> [Sat Jan  3 20:56:26] pmproxy(1683070) Info: pmproxy caught SIGTERM
> [Sat Jan  3 20:56:26] pmproxy(1683070) Info: pmproxy Shutdown
> 
> Log finished Sat Jan  3 20:56:26 2026
> --- start systemctl status for pmproxy.service ---
> * pmproxy.service - Proxy for Performance Metrics Collector Daemon
>      Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/system/pmproxy.service; disabled; preset: disabled)
>     Drop-In: /usr/lib/systemd/system/service.d
>              `-10-timeout-abort.conf
>      Active: inactive (dead) since Sat 2026-01-03 20:56:26 UTC; 19s ago
>    Duration: 821ms
>  Invocation: 1f73a5ccbbc24893b394f63f4186618a
>        Docs: man:pmproxy(1)
>     Process: 1683070 ExecStart=/usr/libexec/pcp/services/pmproxy start-systemd (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
>    Main PID: 1683070 (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
>    Mem peak: 3.9M
>         CPU: 74ms
> --- end systemctl status for pmproxy.service ---
> --- start journalctl entries in last minute for -u pmproxy ---
> Hint: You are currently not seeing messages from other users and the system.
>       Users in groups 'adm', 'systemd-journal', 'wheel' can see all messages.
>       Pass -q to turn off this notice.
> No journal files were opened due to insufficient permissions.
> --- end recent journalctl entries for -u pmproxy ---
